title: zizania aquatica subsp. angustifolia
intro: Zizania aquatica subsp. angustifolia, also known as wild rice, is a type of aquatic grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and relieving digestive issues. The health benefits of Zizania aquatica subsp. angustifolia include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood sugar control, and support cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of wild rice typically involve decoctions or infusions of the dried plant, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or topical applications for various health concerns.
